phase3_findings.description: Generic helper-attribution mechanism untested vs a hardcoded-list regression; all fixture helpers are real.
Misattributed collaborators call to resolve-implement-trigger.sh (actually in sourced authorize-actor.sh); sourced-helper limitation undisclosed.

fix_decisions.evidence: Added synthetic novel-basename helper test; mutation-checked (general loop -> 1, hardcoded-list regression -> 0 -> RED).

Verified collaborators call is in authorize-actor.sh:72 (sourced by resolve-*-trigger.sh, not named in a job body). Corrected attribution + disclosed single-level/sourced limitation. Comprehensively verified (walked all 24 named basenames + children) that no recognized-family endpoint is reachable only transitively.
Verified FALSE — post-issue-comment.sh only comment-mentions them (lines 13, 44), does not invoke. Excluded from the disclosure.

phase3_findings.description: 'any future helper is too' overclaims — walk resolves scripts/ only; a future lib/ job-invoked helper would be unattributed. Latent, not live.
scripts/-only resolution: a recognized-family lib/ helper is fail-OPEN, mislabeled 'fail-safe'. Not live (only lib/efficiency-trace.sh named, no gh api call).
Optional: union-merge not tested with both inline+helper unmet; over-detection per-family; vendored-path form untested. Non-blocking hardening.
